gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Unbounded interval accumulation allows single caller to extract 1.5% of entire TitanX burn pool as incentive after ~50 days of inactivity
candidate.claimed_root_cause: _calculateIntervalsTitanXBurn caps amountAllocated at totalTitanXBurn when missed intervals accumulate; incentive in swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn is computed as amountAllocated * 150 / 10000 with no secondary cap, enabling extraction of 1.5% of the full pool in one call
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged attacker extracts ~5.85e27 TitanX (1.5% of totalTitanXBurn = 390e27) in a single call after ~50 days of no swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn activity

live_preconditions.name: contract TitanX ERC20 balance >= totalTitanXBurn
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT CONFIRMED — live_context erc20 balances only show WETH; TitanX balance not explicitly queried
live_preconditions.source: live_context field balances.erc20 (incomplete — TitanX not listed)

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Accumulates missed intervals linearly (amountPerInterval * missedIntervals) and caps _totalAmountForInterval at totalTitanXBurn with no per-call ceiling below the full pool

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (set by modifier to capped value) and computes incentive = amountAllocated * INCENTIVE_FEE / BPS_DENOM with no secondary cap; transfers incentive to msg.sender at line 254
Modifier runs before function body; writes titanXIntervalsBurn[_lastInterval].amountAllocated = _amountAllocated (the capped value) which is then read by swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
INCENTIVE_FEE=150, BPS_DENOM=10_000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes — all confirmed in source; live DAILY_ALLOCATION_TITAN_X_BURNING=200 bps
IntervalAlreadyBurned guard checks amountBurned != 0 for the current interval only; does not limit amountAllocated size or incentive magnitude

attacker_path.actor: any EOA, no role or privilege required
attacker_inputs: _deadline: any valid future uint32 timestamp (e.g. block.timestamp + 300)
call_sequence: 1. Wait until block.timestamp >= lastBurnedTitanXIntervalStartTimestamp + ~50 days (7199 missed 10-minute intervals) with no intervening swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn calls

4. _calculateIntervalsTitanXBurn(timeElapsed) computes missedIntervals >= 7199, additionalAmount overflows the normal per-interval budget, cap branch sets _totalAmountForInterval = uint128(totalTitanXBurn)
6. Function body: amountAllocated = totalTitanXBurn; incentive = totalTitanXBurn * 150 / 10000
7. TransferHelper.safeTransfer(TITAN_X_ADDRESS, msg.sender, incentive) sends ~5.85e27 TitanX to attacker
state_before: contract holds TitanX >= totalTitanXBurn (implied by accounting; must be confirmed via cast call)
state_after: titanXToSwapAndBurn = amountAllocated - incentive swapped for Torus and burned

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX ERC20 token held by TorusBuyAndProcess contract
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: ~5.85e27 TitanX (1.5% of totalTitanXBurn = 390e27) in a single call
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol burn pool loses 1.5% of its entire TitanX reserve to a single caller instead of distributing it as normal per-interval incentives (~1.5% of 1/144th of 2% daily = negligible per call)
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Normal entitlement per call is incentive on one interval's allocation: totalTitanXBurn * 200/10000 / 144 * 150/10000 ≈ 8.1e20 TitanX. Attacker extracts ~5.85e27 TitanX — approximately 7200x the normal single-call entitlement — because the accumulation cap sets amountAllocated to the full pool with no secondary incentive ceiling

setup: Record titanX.balanceOf(TorusBuyAndProcess) to confirm contract holds TitanX >= totalTitanXBurn
vm.warp(block.timestamp + 51 days) to accumulate >= 7199 missed intervals
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If titanX.balanceOf(TorusBuyAndProcess) < totalTitanXBurn before the call, the safeTransfer will revert and the finding does not hold. If amountAllocated is not capped at totalTitanXBurn (e.g. due to intermediate calls reducing totalTitanXBurn), increase warp duration or adjust expected value.
